evidence.quote: The charge in Figure 3 is referred to as Design 1... The liner is 1mm thick and made from copper. The liner has a cone shape with the top angle being 60°... The main charge is RDX-based explosive JH-2
Wang, F.; Jiang, J.; Men, J... Investigation on shaped charge jet density gradient for metal matrix composites... [27]

Design 2 contains a double-layer metal liner. The inner layer is the active liner... uniform thickness of 2mm... Al particles about 15-45 μm in size were deposited on the substrate by cold spraying... The inactive outer layer is 1mm machined Cu.
Design 3 differs from Design 2 in that the outer Cu liner is half the original height.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: The FASTCAM high-speed photography device has a shooting frequency of 100,000 fps... This trio of steel-aluminum-aluminum plates was repeated five times to give 15 plates in total... Neighboring plates were spaced 20mm apart
the average velocity is 3413, 3267, 3467 m/s for design 1, 2, and 3, respectively.

issues.claim: Jet tip velocities captured (3467 m/s for Design 3)
issues.reason: Text specifies this as 'average velocity' over the target pack, not instantaneous 'tip velocity' (which is ~7950 m/s in simulation). Minor terminology precision issue.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Change 'Jet tip velocities' to 'Average jet velocities' to match the text precisely.

evidence.quote: Perforation, deformation, and deflection were measured for each plate. Figure 9 shows how the impact created by design n is evaluated, where cn and sn are the areas... and the deflection yn

total perforation ranks in the order of design 2 > design 3 > design 1, and total deformation ranks in the order of design 3 > design 1 > design 2... aftereffect of the three designs can be ranked as E3 > E2 > E1

The smooth particle hydrodynamics (SPH) algorithm is employed... A uniform mesh of 0.3mm SPH grids is used globally in Autodyn-3D... liner materials (Cu and Al) adopt the Johnson-Cook model with shock EOS... explosive 8701 employs the JWL equation of state

For design 2... The Cu jet is firstly rapidly extruded to form the tip... ensuing Al jet gradually moves to the tail to wrap the Cu tail. In design 3... the Cu jet is entirely wrapped by Al from the tip to the tail.
